leadText: Dragon Pulse redirects here. For the energy from White—Victini and Zekrom and Black—Victini and Reshiram known as "Dragon Pulse" in Japanese, see Dragon Force. Dragon Pulse (Japanese: りゅうのはどう Dragon Pulse) is a damage-dealing Dragon-type move introduced in Generation IV. It is: TM59 in Generation IV and in Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl. TM34 in Pokémon: Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee!. TR62 in Pokémon Sword and Shield. TM115 in Pokémon Scarlet and Violet. TM075 in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generations IV and V 1.2 Generation VI onwards 1.3 Generation VIII 1.3.1 Pokémon Legends: Arceus 1.4 Generation IX 1.4.1 Pokémon Legends: Z-A 1.5 Other effects Effect [ edit | edit source ] Generations IV and V [ edit | edit source ] Dragon Pulse inflicts damage and can hit non-adjacent opponents in Triple Battles . Generation VI onwards [ edit | edit source ] Dragon Pulse's base power is reduced from 90 to 85. Additionally, its power is boosted by 50% if the user has the Ability Mega Launcher . Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Arceus [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Legends: Arceus , the move bypasses accuracy checks to always hit. Pokémon Legends: Arceus Category Special PP 10 Agile Strong Power 80 60 100 Accuracy —% —% —% Action speed modifier Self 0 0 +5 Target 0 +3 0 Additional effect ? ? ? ? ? ? Never misses Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A Category Special Accuracy 100% Standard Plus Move Power 85 102 Cooldown 8s Move wind-up time 20 frames Effect duration —s Additional effect — — — None Other effects [ edit | edit source ] A Poipole that knows this move will evolve into Naganadel when leveled up.
